feedback.text: I want to say thank you for hosting this open forum where consumers have a voice to express their concerns with the digital media market to those who hear us and care for us. To the extent of the protections in place for consumers, I would like to request that further protection is added to protect consumers' rights to keep digital media after publishers end their support. As it stands, video game companies have the capacity to effectively "rent" video games under the guise of selling complete ownership of a title, with the ability to then take that video game away once the publisher decides they do not want to retain support any longer. This is hostile to the consumer's right of ownership, and muddies the distinction between leased software and game ownership. My personal belief is that if you buy a game, you should own it, forever. And for that to become the reality for consumers, more protections need to be put in place to ensure that studios and publishers plan for the "End of Life" phase of their games, such that games can continue to exist in a state that is complete in function and form to that of the product sold to the consumer in the first place. No consumer should have to wake up one day to find that their ability to access digital media and games that they've purchased, suddenly be revoked. Thank you for your time.
